applied.name: Pappadeaux Seafood Kitchen
applied.old: Houston Cajun seafood chain that locals swear by. The crawfish etouffee is rich and proper, the blackened redfish is perfectly charred, and the portions are Texas-sized. Multiple locations across the city. The Pappas family knows how to feed Houston.
applied.next: Louisiana-style seafood chain known for fried shrimp, crawfish, and stuffed redfish. Large portions, lively atmosphere, and a reliable happy hour across multiple Houston-area locations.

applied.name: Pho Saigon
applied.old: Bellaire pho institution that has been serving clean, deeply flavored broth to Houston Viet community for decades. The pho tai is the standard -- rare beef that cooks in the hot broth -- and the portions are enormous for the price. Cash only. No frills. Perfect pho.
applied.next: Classic Houston Vietnamese pho destination with rich, long-simmered broth and generous bowls. Family-run, consistent, and a Gulf Coast Vietnamese-community staple.

applied.name: The Pit Room
applied.old: Michelin Bib Gourmand BBQ in Montrose with brisket, sausage, and pulled pork that rival the best in the state. The breakfast tacos (served 7-10:30am) are a sleeper hit. The covered patio at The Patio next door is perfect for beers and live music. Two locations now, but Montrose on Richmond Ave is the original.
applied.next: Michelin Bib Gourmand Texas BBQ from pitmaster Bramwell Tripp — brisket, sausage, and pulled pork that rival the best in the state. The breakfast tacos (served 7-10:30am) are a sleeper hit. Multiple Houston locations.

applied.name: Ramen Tatsu-Ya
applied.old: Austin's cult ramen hit brought to Montrose with rich, deeply flavored tonkotsu broth that's been simmering for 18+ hours. The OG Tonkotsu is the essential order, and the vegan options are surprisingly excellent. Lines form nightly and for good reason. The best bowl of ramen in Houston, full stop.
applied.next: Austin's cult ramen export brought to Houston with rich, deeply flavored tonkotsu broth that's been simmering for 18+ hours. The OG Tonkotsu is the essential order, and the vegan options are surprisingly excellent. One of Houston's best bowls of ramen.

applied.name: Gringo's Mexican Kitchen
applied.old: Beloved suburban Tex-Mex chain with locations across greater Houston. The green chili queso is habit-forming, the fajitas are sizzling, and the margaritas are generously poured. A Texas original that's the go-to for families from Katy to League City.
applied.next: Beloved Houston Tex-Mex chain with locations across greater Houston. The green chili queso is habit-forming, the fajitas are sizzling, and the margaritas are generously poured. A Texas original that's the go-to for families.

applied.name: Peli Peli
applied.old: Houston's South African restaurant bringing Cape Town flavor to the Galleria with a whimsical tree-in-the-dining-room interior. The menu draws from African, Dutch, and Malay influences — peri-peri chicken, bobotie, and big-game inspired plates.
applied.next: Houston's South African restaurant bringing Cape Town flavor with a whimsical tree-in-the-dining-room interior. The menu draws from African, Dutch, and Malay influences — peri-peri chicken, bobotie, and big-game inspired plates.

applied.name: Dish Society
applied.old: Aaron Lyons' local and sustainable cafe concept with a Memorial Drive location. Farm-to-table breakfast, lunch, and dinner with scratch-made biscuits, local eggs, and a seasonal menu that genuinely changes. A reliable family and brunch spot.
applied.next: Aaron Lyons' local and sustainable cafe concept. Farm-to-table breakfast, lunch, and dinner with scratch-made biscuits, local eggs, and a seasonal menu that genuinely changes. A reliable family and brunch spot across multiple Houston-area locations.

applied.name: Becks Prime
applied.old: Houston's best patio burger, set inside Memorial Park under giant live oaks. Mesquite-grilled prime burgers, dogs, and milkshakes after a run on the loop. A Houston rite of passage for weekend mornings.
applied.next: Houston's favorite patio burger. Mesquite-grilled prime burgers, dogs, and milkshakes — a local burger rite of passage. The Memorial Park location sits under giant live oaks and is the classic post-run spot.

applied.name: Paperboy
applied.old: East Austin all-day cafe in an airy, plant-filled space serving inventive breakfast and brunch dishes with fresh, health-conscious ingredients.
applied.next: Austin all-day cafe in airy, plant-filled spaces serving inventive breakfast and brunch dishes with fresh, health-conscious ingredients.

applied.name: Cosmic Coffee + Beer Garden
applied.old: South Congress coffee shop and beer garden with a massive shaded patio, food truck lot, and live music. Morning coffee transitions to afternoon craft beer and evening cocktails. Multiple food trucks rotate through. The chickens wandering the lot are very Austin.
applied.next: Austin coffee shop and beer garden with massive shaded patios, food truck lots, and live music. Morning coffee transitions to afternoon craft beer and evening cocktails. Multiple food trucks rotate through. The chickens wandering the lot are very Austin.

applied.name: Oseyo
applied.old: East Austin Korean restaurant bringing refined Korean flavors to Cesar Chavez. Bibimbap, Korean fried chicken, and banchan are elevated with quality ingredients and careful technique. The cocktail program adds Korean-inspired drinks. One of Austin's best Korean dining experiences.
applied.next: Austin Korean restaurant bringing refined Korean flavors — bibimbap, Korean fried chicken, and banchan elevated with quality ingredients and careful technique. The cocktail program adds Korean-inspired drinks. One of Austin's best Korean dining experiences.

applied.name: Cenote
applied.old: East Cesar Chavez coffee shop in a converted house with a massive shaded patio. Craft coffee, pastries, and light bites in a peaceful garden setting. The morning coffee ritual here is a very East Austin experience. Free WiFi and plenty of shade.
applied.next: Austin coffee shop in converted houses with massive shaded patios. Craft coffee, pastries, and light bites in peaceful garden settings. Free WiFi and plenty of shade — the local morning coffee ritual.

applied.name: Jack Allen's Kitchen
applied.old: Texas comfort food with a farm-to-table focus — chicken fried steak, Gulf shrimp, and biscuits from a chef who sources from Texas ranches. Multiple locations serve the same quality. The brunch is hearty and the happy hour is a value. A Hill Country comfort food staple.
applied.next: Texas comfort food with a farm-to-table focus — chicken fried steak, Gulf shrimp, and biscuits from a chef who sources from Texas ranches. Multiple Austin-area locations serve the same quality. The brunch is hearty and the happy hour is a value. A Hill Country comfort food staple.

applied.name: Loro
applied.old: The East Austin location of the Tyson Cole + Aaron Franklin collaboration. Same smoked brisket with Thai herbs, same coconut curry, new neighborhood. The patio is spacious and the bar program is strong. All the Loro magic in East Austin.
applied.next: Tyson Cole + Aaron Franklin's Asian-smokehouse collaboration — smoked brisket with Thai herbs, coconut curry, and a strong bar program. The original East Austin flagship launched the concept; now multiple locations across Austin. Michelin recognized.

applied.name: Uchi
applied.old: The original Uchi on South Lamar — Tyson Cole's James Beard-winning Japanese restaurant in a converted bungalow. The hama chili, machi cure, and omakase are legendary. The restaurant that put Austin on the national fine dining map.
applied.next: Tyson Cole's James Beard Award-winning Japanese restaurant — the hama chili, machi cure, and omakase are legendary. The original South Lamar location put Austin on the national fine dining map, with a second location at The Domain.

applied.name: Lustre Pearl
applied.old: One of the original Rainey Street bungalow bars that helped transform the neighborhood. Massive patio, cheap beer, and a casual party vibe. Lustre Pearl is the OG Rainey Street experience that started it all.
applied.next: One of the original Rainey Street bungalow bars that helped transform the neighborhood. Massive patio, cheap beer, and a casual party vibe. The OG Rainey Street experience that started it all.

applied.name: Ramen Tatsu-Ya
applied.old: The original North Austin outpost of Austin's most beloved ramen shop, serving tonkotsu, chicken, and vegan broths.
applied.next: Austin's most beloved ramen shop, serving tonkotsu, chicken, and vegan broths across multiple Austin locations. Started the city's ramen scene and still the benchmark.

applied.name: Rodeo Goat
applied.old: Craft burger joint that originated in Fort Worth in 2012 (Shannon Wynne and Keith Schlabs) and expanded to the Dallas Design District in 2015. Known for creative burgers, a strong cocktail program, and an extensive craft beer list in a high-energy casual setting. Several North Texas locations.
applied.next: Craft burger concept that originated in Fort Worth in 2012 (Shannon Wynne and Keith Schlabs) and expanded across North Texas. Known for creative burgers, a strong cocktail program, and an extensive craft beer list in a high-energy casual setting.

applied.name: Serious Pie
applied.old: Tom Douglas's original pizzeria in downtown Seattle. Wood-fired, artisan pizzas with blistered crusts and seasonal toppings. One of the only places in Seattle slinging crisp Connecticut-style pies.
applied.next: Tom Douglas's pizzeria — wood-fired, artisan pizzas with blistered crusts and seasonal toppings. One of the only places in Seattle slinging crisp Connecticut-style pies. Two locations across downtown Seattle.

applied.name: Monsoon
applied.old: Capitol Hill contemporary Vietnamese cornerstone on 19th Ave E for 25+ years. Co-founders Eric and Sophie Banh's flagship — authentic Vietnamese with a modern twist. Bar, rooftop patio, and elevated regional dishes.
applied.next: Contemporary Vietnamese cornerstone for 25+ years. Co-founders Eric and Sophie Banh — authentic Vietnamese with a modern twist. Elevated regional dishes, bar program, and a loyal Seattle-area following. Capitol Hill flagship plus Bellevue.

applied.name: Japonessa Sushi Cocina
applied.old: Latin-Japanese fusion sushi next to Pike Place Market. Creative rolls, sashimi, late-night lounge, and happy hour. A reliable Seattle downtown sushi fix with a lively bar scene.
applied.next: Latin-Japanese fusion sushi near Pike Place Market. Creative rolls, sashimi, late-night lounge, and happy hour. A reliable Seattle sushi fix with a lively bar scene. Two Seattle locations.

applied.name: Piroshky Piroshky
applied.old: The line out the door at Pike Place — authentic hand-made Russian piroshky since 1992. Beef & onion, smoked salmon paté, apple cinnamon roll. A Seattle must-do.
applied.next: Authentic hand-made Russian piroshky since 1992 — beef & onion, smoked salmon paté, apple cinnamon roll. The line out the door at Pike Place is legendary; a second Ballard location opens up some breathing room. A Seattle must-do.

applied.name: Tavolàta
applied.old: Ethan Stowell's second Tavolàta — Capitol Hill Pike-Pine corridor Italian pasta house. Hand-made rigatoni, gnocchi alla Romana, and a long communal table. Sister to Tavolata Belltown.
applied.next: Ethan Stowell's Italian pasta house — hand-made rigatoni, gnocchi alla Romana, and long communal tables. The Belltown original plus a Capitol Hill Pike-Pine sister location.

applied.name: Wild Ginger
applied.old: Downtown pan-Asian destination since 1989 — satay bar, Burmese, Thai, and Chinese regional dishes. Second location in Bellevue. A Seattle benchmark for Asian fine dining with longevity.
applied.next: Pan-Asian destination since 1989 — satay bar, Burmese, Thai, and Chinese regional dishes. Downtown flagship plus a Bellevue second. A Seattle benchmark for Asian fine dining with longevity.

applied.name: Dough Zone
applied.old: Xiaolongbao specialist with thin-skinned soup dumplings, pan-fried Q-bao pork buns, and handmade noodles. Originally Bellevue, the ID location is always busy.
applied.next: Xiaolongbao specialist with thin-skinned soup dumplings, pan-fried Q-bao pork buns, and handmade noodles. Originally Bellevue, now with a Chinatown-ID location — both are always busy.

applied.name: Pretty Bird
applied.old: Chef Viet Pham's Nashville-style hot chicken restaurant has become a Salt Lake sensation. The perfectly crispy fried chicken comes at customizable spice levels on a brioche bun. Multiple locations now open including Sugar House and Park City.
applied.next: Chef Viet Pham's Nashville-style hot chicken has become a Salt Lake sensation. Perfectly crispy fried chicken at customizable spice levels on a brioche bun, now across multiple SLC-area locations.

applied.name: Kiitos Brewing
applied.old: Finnish-inspired craft brewery (kiitos means 'thank you' in Finnish) producing creative, small-batch beers in the Granary District. Known for experimental flavors and fruit-forward sours. Also has a second taproom in Sugar House.
applied.next: Finnish-inspired craft brewery (kiitos means 'thank you' in Finnish) producing creative, small-batch beers. Known for experimental flavors and fruit-forward sours, with multiple SLC-area taprooms.

applied.name: Pago
applied.old: The flagship restaurant of the Pago Restaurant Group, pioneering farm-to-table dining in SLC since 2009. Located in the historic Woodman Building in the charming 9th & 9th neighborhood. Chef-driven seasonal menus highlight relationships with local farms and artisan producers.
applied.next: The flagship restaurant of the Pago Restaurant Group, pioneering farm-to-table dining in SLC since 2009. Chef-driven seasonal menus highlight relationships with local farms and artisan producers. Two locations across Salt Lake.

applied.name: Manoli's
applied.old: Mediterranean restaurant in The Avenues from chef Manoli Katsanevas serving Greek and coastal Mediterranean dishes. Fresh seafood, lamb, and spreads in a warm, neighborhood setting. Salt Lake Magazine dining award winner. The octopus and lamb chops are signatures.
applied.next: Chef Manoli Katsanevas' Greek and coastal Mediterranean restaurant — fresh seafood, lamb, and spreads in warm, neighborhood settings. Salt Lake Magazine dining award winner. The octopus and lamb chops are signatures.

applied.name: Hearth and Hill
applied.old: Sugar House location from the team behind Urban Hill (fine dining) and Hearth and Hill Park City. Positioned between casual and upscale — perfect for weekend brunch, lunch, or date night. Seasonal American menu with craft cocktails.
applied.next: From the team behind Urban Hill (fine dining) — positioned between casual and upscale, perfect for weekend brunch, lunch, or date night. Seasonal American menu with craft cocktails across multiple Utah locations.

applied.name: Tuk Tuk Thai
applied.old: Thai restaurant with locations in West Valley, Draper, and the Marmalade neighborhood. Pad thai, curries, and noodle dishes at accessible prices. A solid neighborhood Thai option.
applied.next: Thai restaurant with multiple Salt Lake-area locations. Pad thai, curries, and noodle dishes at accessible prices. A solid neighborhood Thai option.

applied.name: Table X
applied.old: The place to celebrate a big anniversary in Salt Lake City. Multi-course tasting menus with local ingredients and precise technique. The most intimate fine dining experience in the SLC metro. Worth the drive to Millcreek.
applied.next: The place to celebrate a big anniversary in Salt Lake City. Multi-course tasting menus with local ingredients and precise technique. The most intimate fine dining experience in the SLC metro.

applied.name: Slackwater
applied.old: Wood-fired pizza and craft beer in historic downtown Ogden. The Margherita is excellent and the beer selection spans Utah breweries. Worth the drive north from SLC for pizza lovers.
applied.next: Wood-fired pizza and craft beer from a Utah brewpub chain. The Margherita is excellent and the beer selection spans Utah breweries. Multiple Salt Lake-area locations — the original Ogden taproom is the flagship.

applied.name: Aroon Thai
applied.old: Thai restaurant with a new location in Cottonwood Heights, well-regarded with excellent reviews. Authentic Thai curries and noodle dishes.
applied.next: Well-regarded Thai kitchen with excellent reviews across multiple SLC-area locations. Authentic Thai curries and noodle dishes.

applied.name: Caputo's
applied.old: Holladay location of the beloved Italian deli. Same great muffuletta and specialty Italian products.
applied.next: Beloved Italian deli and market with multiple Utah locations. Same great muffuletta and specialty Italian products at each site.
